q.t: Which one of the following is an essential federal feature of Indian polity?

so.t:
A written constitution: The details of the federal system of government are generally spelt out in a written constitution, which is considered to be supreme and which is also the source of the power of both sets of government. There are two sets of government created by the Indian Constitution: one for the entire nation called the union government (central government) and one for each unit or State called the State government. Both of these have a constitutional status and identified areas of activity. If there is any dispute about which powers come under the control of the union and which under the States, this can be resolved by the Judiciary based on the constitutional provisions.
* States not indestructible: Unlike in other federations, the states in India have no right to territorial integrity. The Parliament can by unilateral action change the area, boundaries or name of any state. It is a unitary feature of the Indian polity.
* Integrated judiciary: The Indian Constitution has established an integrated judicial system with the Supreme Court at the top and the state high courts below it. This single system of courts enforces both the Central laws as well as the state laws. It is a unitary feature of the Indian polity
Flexibility of the Constitution: The process of constitutional amendment is less rigid than what is found in other federations. The bulk of the Constitution can be amended by the unilateral action of the Parliament, either by a simple majority or by a special majority
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Rajya Sabha passes a resolution in ‘National Interest’, supported by two-thirds of the members present and voting.
2. A proclamation of national emergency is in operation
3. When the President’s rule is imposed in a State.
4. The Parliament has to implement international agreements
In how many of the above cases, Parliament becomes empowered to enact laws on a subject mentioned in the State List of the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India?

so.t:
The Constitution empowers the Parliament to make laws on any matter enumerated in the State List under the following five extraordinary circumstances:
If the Rajya Sabha declares that it is necessary for the national interest that Parliament should make laws concerning goods and services tax or a matter in the State List. then the Parliament becomes competent to make laws on that matter. Such a resolution must be supported by two-thirds of the members present and voting. So, point I is correct.
The Parliament acquires the power to legislate for goods and services tax or matters in the State List, while a proclamation of national emergency is in operation. The laws become inoperative on the expiration of six months after the emergency has ceased to operate. So, point 2 is correct.
When the President's rule is imposed in a State, the Parliament becomes empowered to make laws concerning any matter in the State List about that State. A law made so by the Parliament continues to be operative even after the President's rule. But, such a law can be repealed, altered or re-enacted by the State legislature. So, point 3 is correct.
The Parliament can make laws on any matter in the State List for implementing international treaties, agreements or conventions. Some examples of laws enacted under the above provision are the United Nations (Privileges and Immunities) Act, 1947; Geneva Convention Act, 1960, Anti-Hijacking Act, 1982 and legislations relating to the environment and TRIPS. So, point 4 is correct.
When the legislatures of two or more States pass resolutions requesting the Parliament to enact laws on a matter in the State List, then the Parliament can make laws for regulating that matter. A law so enacted applies only to those States which have passed the resolutions
So, in all four of the above cases, Parliament becomes empowered to enact laws on a subject mentioned in the State List of the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
Relevance: The Indian Constitution empowers the Parliament to make laws on any matter enumerated in the Stace List under extraordinary circumstances.

q.t:
With reference to the Inter-State Council, consider the following statements:
1. It is set up by the President of India to serve the public interest.
2. It is chaired by the Prime Minister of India.
3. It was established in 1990 based on the recommendations of the Sarkaria Commission.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
Article 263 of the Constitution of India provides for the Inter-State Council to effect coordination between the States and between the Centre and States. The President has the power to establish such a council if at any time it appears to him that the public interest would be served by its establishment. So, statement I is correct.
The Inter-state council is chaired by the Prime Minister. Other members of the council include Chief Ministers of all the states, Chief Ministers of Union Territories having Legislative Assemblies. Administrators of Union Territories not having Legislative Assemblies, Governors of States under President's rule. Six Central Cabinet Ministers, including the Home Minister, to be nominated by the Prime Minister. So, statement 2 is correct.
* The Sarkaria Commission on Centre-State Relations (1983-88) made a strong case for the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council under Article 263 of the Constitution. In pursuance of the above recommendations of the Sarkaria Commission, the Janata Dal Government headed by V. P. Singh established the Inter-State Council in 1990. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t: . The term “marble cake federalism”, is used in the context of:

so.t:
Marble cake federalism is a bakery metaphor often used to describe the model of cooperative federalism. This model of federalism holds that the Local. State and National Governments do not act in separate spheres, but instead have interrelated policy goals and administrative duties.
In marble cake federalism there will be an intermingling of all levels of government in policies and programming
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. To have village governance with participatory democracy
2. To provide self-rule for the tribal population
3. To prevent Panchayats at the higher level from assuming the powers
4. To safeguard the traditions and customs of tribal communities
How many of the above is/are the objective(s) of the “Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled Areas) Act (PESA), 1996”?

so.t:
The Parliament enacted the "Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled Areas) Act, 1996, popularly known as the PESA Act or the Extension Act, to extend the provisions of Part IX of the constitution to the Fifth Schedule areas. The objectives of the PESA Act are as follows:
To extend the provisions of Part IX of the Constitution relating to the Panchayats to the scheduled areas with certain modifications.
* To safeguard and preserve the traditions and customs of tribal communities. o To have village governance with participatory democracy and to make the Gram Sabha a
nucleus of all activities To evolve a suitable administrative framework consistent with traditional practices
* To safeguard and preserve the traditions and customs of tribal communities
To provide self-rule for the bulk of the tribal population
o To empower Panchayats at the appropriate levels with specific powers conducive to tribal requirements
To prevent Panchayats at the higher level from assuming the powers and authority of Panchayats at the lower level of the Gram Satha.
So, all four of the above are the objective(s) of the "Panchayats (Extension to the Scheduled
Areas) Act (PESA), 1996".
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Issuance of voter ID cards
2. Allocating symbols to Political parties
3. Supervising elections of the Panchayat Raj Institutions
4. Implementing Model Code of Conduct
Which of the above functions are performed by the Election Commission of India?

so.t:
Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for an independent Election Commission (EC) for the 'superintendence, direction and control of electoral roll and the conduct of elections' in India.
A voter ID card is a document that ascertains one's identity as an adult citizen and is primarily used in casting one's vote in state and general elections. It is issued by the Election Commission, which is also known as the Electoral Photo ID Card (EPIC). So, point I is correct.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) accords recognition to political parties and allots symbols to each of them. It also grants political parties the status of national or state parties based on their poll performance. So, point 2 is correct.
State Election Commissions (and not ECI) supervises and conducts elections of Urban Local Bodies and other Panchayat Raj Institutions (PRIs) as per the procedures laid down in State laws. So, point 3 is not correct.
The Election Commission of India implements a Model Code of Conduct for parties and candidates. It determines the code of conduct to be observed by the parties and the candidates at the time of elections. So, point 4 is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the District Council established under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India:
1. All of its members are nominated by the Governor of the respective State.
2. It can make regulations for the control of money lending and trading by non-tribals with the assent of the Governor.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
The Constitution, under the Sixth Schedule, contains special provisions for the administration of Tribal Areas in the four north-eastern states of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram.
Each Autonomous District has a District Council consisting of 30 members, of whom four are nominated by the Governor and the remaining 26 are elected based on adult franchise. The elected members hold office for a term of five years (unless the council is dissolved earlier) and nominated members hold office at the pleasure of the Governor. So, statement I is not correct.
The District Council can establish, construct or manage primary schools, dispensaries, markets, ferries, fisheries, roads and so on in the district. It can also make regulations for the control of money lending and trading by non-tribals. But, such regulations require the assent of the Governor.
So, statement 2 is correct.
The District and Regional Councils are empowered to assess and collect land revenue and to impose certain specified taxes.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
Relevance: Leh Apex Body and the Kargil Democratic Alliance have been demanding full-fledged statehood for Ladakh, constitutional safeguards under the Sixth Schedule of the Indian constitution.

opt.t: The President may create a new autonomous district in the areas occupied by Schedule Tribes.
This would create regional councils that can make laws on land and forests.
This would authorise land acquisition by the government.
Acts of Parliament will apply with specified modifications and exceptions as deemed by the President.

q.t: If a particular area is brought under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India, which one of the following is not the consequence of it?

so.t:
The Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India deals with the administration of the tribal areas in the four northeastern states of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram.
The Governor, in the areas occupied by Schedule Tribes, may create a new autonomous district, increase the area of any autonomous district, diminish the area of any autonomous district, and unite two or more autonomous districts.
* These autonomous districts have district councils and regional councils. The district council has the power to make laws in the following matters:
Deals with the allotment, occupation or use, or the setting apart, of land, other than any land which is a reserved forest for purposes likely to promote the interests of the inhabitants of any village or town;
They can make laws on certain specified matters like land, forests, canal water, shifting cultivation, village administration, inheritance of property, marriage and divorce, social customs and so on.
Authorise land acquisition, provided that nothing in such laws shall prevent the compulsory acquisition of any land, whether occupied or unoccupied, for public purposes by the Government of the State.
The Acts of Parliament or the State Legislature do not apply to autonomous districts and autonomous regions or apply with specified modifications and exceptions as deemed by the President.
Therefore, option (a) is the answer

opt.t: It is a form of decentralization that seeks to create an independent level of authority of government with functions and responsibilities.
It is a constitutional provision that grants overarching authority to the central government.
It is the distribution of voting power within an organization, allowing leaders from various locations to participate.
It involves the consolidation of diverse states into a unified political system.

q.t: In the context of Indian Polity, devolution of power refers to which one of the following?

so.t:
Devolution of power:
It is a form of decentralization that seeks to create an independent level of authority of government with functions and responsibilities.
It is an arrangement for Central or State Governments to relinquish some of their functions to the new units of government that are outside their control.
This can be achieved by providing for it in the Constitution itself or by ordinary law of the land.
Devolution usually occurs through conventional statutes rather than through a change in a country's Constitution; thus, unitary systems of government that have devolved powers in this manner are still considered unitary rather than federal systems, because the Central Government can withdraw the powers of the subnational authorities at any time.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the Tribes Advisory Council (TAC), consider the following statements:
1. It is a constitutional body formed under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution.
2. Three-fourths of its members are the representatives of Scheduled Tribes in the State Legislative Assembly.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Tribes Advisory Councils (TACs) are constitutional bodies formed under the Fifth Schedule to deal with the welfare and advancement of scheduled tribes in states. TAC is established under Article 244(1) of the Constitution of India in each State having Scheduled Areas therein and, if the President so directs, also in any State having Scheduled Tribes. So, statement I is correct.
Accordingly, TAC has been constituted in ten states having Scheduled Areas therein namely Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, Himachal Pradesh, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Odisha and Rajasthan.
Further, the States of West Bengal, Tamil Nadu and Uttarakhand do not have any notified Scheduled Area also have Tribes Advisory Council constituted therein. Accordingly, there is no State (mandated to establish TAC), which has not constituted TAC.
Each TAC is to consist of 20 members, three-fourths of whom are to be the representatives of the Scheduled Tribes (STs) in the State Legislative Assembly. This applies provided that if the number of representatives of STs in the State Assembly is less than the number of seats in TAC to be filled by such representatives, the remaining seats shall be filled by other members of those tribes. So, statement 2 is correct.
The Tribal Councils have to advise on such matters about the welfare and advancement of the Scheduled Tribes in the State as may be referred to them by the Governor.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. The members of a State Public Service Commission are appointed and removed by the President.
2. The Union Public Service Commission can serve the needs of a State at the request of the State Governor.
3. The chairperson and members of the Joint State Public Service Commission are appointed by the Governors of the concerned States.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
A State Public Service Commission (SPSC) consists of a Chairman and other members appointed by the Governor of the State. However, the Chairman and members of an SPSC can be removed only by the President. The President can remove them on the same grounds and in the same manner as he can remove a chairman or a member of the UPSC. So, statement I is not correct.
The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) can serve the needs of a state at the request of the State Governor and with the approval of the President. The UPSC assists the States (when requested by two or more states) in framing and operating schemes of joint recruitment for any services for which candidates possessing special qualifications are required. So, statement 2 is correct.
The Parliament can establish a Joint State Public Service Commission (JSPSC) for two or more states at the request of the State Legislatures concerned. The chairperson and members of the JSPSC are appointed by the President. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Unlike some other Union Territories, Puducherry and Delhi have legislative powers
2. Special status conferred on Assam under Article 371B of the Constitution of India
3. Administration of tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India
How many of the above provisions/features show the presence of Asymmetrical Federalism in India?

so.t:
Asymmetrical Federalism:
It refers to a flexible type of Union of States which allows the Government to cut different deals with different States in special matters about them. This method allows the government to grant special status to some units providing them with special powers not enjoyed by other states.
Asymmetrical Federalism in India:
Puducherry and Delhi have legislatures, while some other Union Territories under the Centre do not have legislatures or a Ministerial Council to advise the administrator. Also, the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019 states that there shall be a Legislative Assembly for the Union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. So, point I is correct.
Under Article 371-B, the President is empowered to provide for the creation of a committee of the Assam Legislative Assembly consisting of the members elected from the Tribal Areas of the state and such other members as he may specify. So, point 2 is correct.
The Sixth Schedule to the Constitution of India contains provisions for the administration of tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram. These create autonomous districts and autonomous regions. So, point 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above provisions/features show the presence of Asymmetrical Federalism in India.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statement regarding interstate relations:
1. Article 252 of the constitution provides for the adjudication of inter-state water disputes.
2. A river board is established by the Central government on the request of the state governments concerned to advise them.
3. Until today, the central government has established only three inter-state water dispute tribunals, each successfully adjudicating all disputes assigned to them.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
While Article 252 does empower Parliament to legislate for two or more states by consent, the primary provision for adjudicating inter-state water disputes is found in Article 262 of the Constitution. This article grants Parliament the authority to provide for the adjudication of disputes related to the use, distribution. or control of the waters of inter-state rivers or river valleys. So, Statement I is incorrect.
Statement 2 is correct. While the establishment can be initiated on request or consultation with
State Governments, the ultimate authority to establish the River Board rests with the Central government. Therefore, the statement accurately reflects that the Central government plays a crucial role in the establishment of River Boards.
Statement 3 is incorrect. The central government has established more than three inter-state water
dispute tribunals, and not all of them have successfully adjudicated disputes. The primary mechanism for adjudicating such disputes is through Water Disputes Tribunals constituted under the Inter-State River Water Disputes (ISRWD) Act, 1956. So far, nine water disputes tribunals have been constituted, and five have adjudicated water disputes. These tribunals are dissolved after completing their assigned tasks.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. Freedom of inter-State and intra-State trade can be enforced under Article 32.
2. Article 301 applies to inter-State trade and intercourse as well as intra-State trade, commerce, and intercourse.
3. A bill to impose reasonable restrictions on the freedom of trade, commerce or intercourse within a State shall be introduced in the Legislature of a State with the previous sanction of the President.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
Statement I is incorrect & 2 is correct. Part XIII of the Indian Constitution from Articles 301 to 307 deals with inter-state and intra-state trade, commerce, and intercourse within the territory of India. The freedom of inter-state trade and commerce guaranteed by Article 301 is freedom from all restrictions. The provisions of Article 301 are mandatory and any law violating them is ultra vires, but these provisions are not fundamental rights and are not enforceable under Article 32.
Statement 3 is correct. Article 304(b) in The Constitution states that the Central Government may impose such reasonable restrictions on the freedom of trade, commerce or intercourse with or within that State as may be required in the public interest. It also stated that no Bill or amendment for this purpose shall be introduced in the Legislature of a State without the previous sanction of the President.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
Both Statement-I and Statement- II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: The principle of separation of power promotes constitutionalism in India.
Statement II: Article 246 of the constitution contains provisions dealing with the division of powers between the Union and the States with respect to three lists of schedule VII.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
The principle of separation of power states that power is shared by various branches of government, including the legislature, executive, and judiciary. This is horizontal sharing of power among different organs of government. The government organs work at the same levels to exercise varied powers. Each organ in horizontal power-sharing is a separate entity that keeps the other organ in check. As a result, each organ possesses a check-in ability and promotes the constitutionalism in India. Hence statement I is correct.
The seventh schedule under Article 246 of the constitution deals with the division of powers between the union and the states. It is among different levels of government, such as Union, State, and local government.
It contains three lists Union List, State List and Concurrent List.
The union list details the subjects on which Parliament may make laws while the state list details those under the purview of state legislatures.
The concurrent list on the other hand has subjects in which both Parliament and state legislatures have jurisdiction. It promotes the concept of deepening democracy through federalism and not through horizontal power sharing. Hence, statement 2 is correct

opt.t: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
Both Statement-I and Statement- II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement 1: Article 239AA of the Constitution, introduced by the 69th Amendment Act, 1991, grants special status to Delhi.
Statement 2: The introduction of Article 239AA created a federal model with the Union of India at the Centre and the NCTD at the regional level.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
Statement 1: Article 239AA of the Constitution, introduced by the 69th Amendment Act, 1991, does grant special status to Delhi. This statement is correct.
Statement 2: The introduction of Article 239AA did create a federal model with the Union of India at the Centre and the National Capital Territory of Delhi (NCTD) at the regional level. However, the federal model is a broader concept, and the statement does not directly explain the specific provisions or reasons for granting special status to Delhi.
Therefore, both statements are individually correct, but Statement-ll does not serve as a direct explanation for Statement-l.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. Communication devices including telephones finds mention in the Union List under the 7th schedule.
2. Both the Central and the State Governments have a right to tap phones under Indian Telegraph Act, 1885.
3. According to the Indian Telegraph (Amendment) Rules, 2007, phone tapping orders could be issued only by the Secretary, Union Ministry of Home Affairs.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
* Telephones along with other communication devices find mention under Entry 31 of the Union List.
Hence, statement I is correct.
Both the Central and the State Governments have a right to tap phones under Section 5(2) of Indian Telegraphic Act, 1885. Hence, statement 2 is correct.
In the states, police have the power to tap phones. At the Centre, 10 agencies are authorised to do so: Intelligence Bureau, CBI, Enforcement Directorate, Narcotics Control Bureau, Central Board of Direct Taxes, Directorate of Revenue Intelligence, National Investigation Agency, R&AW, Directorate of Signal Intelligence, and the Delhi Police Commissioner. Tapping by any other agency would be considered illegal.
According to the Rule 419A of the Indian Telegraph (Amendment) Rules, 2007, phone tapping orders could be issued only by the Secretary, Union Ministry of Home Affairs or his State Counterpart. The order has to be conveyed to the service provider in writing; only then can the tapping begin. The competent authority must record reasons for tapping in writing.
However, in the exceptional cases the order may be issued by the lower authorities also which may include officer, not below the rank of a Joint Secretary, to the Government of India or the head or the second senior-most officer of the authorised law enforcement agency at the central level, and by authorised officers, not below the rank of Inspector General of Police, at the state level. Hence, statement 3 is incorrect.
Such order has to be communicated to the competent authority within a specified time period.
Therefore, option b is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding GST (Goods & Services) Appellate Tribunal:
1. The GST Appellate Tribunal is a quasi-judicial body.
2. It will help in reducing Judicial Pendency.
3. Rules for the appointment of the president and members of GST appellate tribunals are notified by the Ministry of Finance.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
The GST Appellate Tribunal is a quasi-judicial body proposed to be established to resolve disputes related to the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India. Hence, Statement I is correct.
The proposed tribunal is expected to help expedite the resolution of disputes related to GST and reduce the burden on the judiciary. Hence, Statement 2 is correct.
It will function a body to hear appeals against orders passed by the GST authorities or the Appellate Authority. The tribunal will be composed of a national bench and various regional benches, headed by a chairperson appointed by the central government. The finance ministry notifies the rules for the appointment of the president and members of GST appellate tribunals. Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (Appointment and Conditions of Service of President and Members) Rules, 2023, defines the rule for appointment and removal of the president and members of the appellate tribunals, their salary, allowances, pension, provident fund, gratuity and leave.
Hence, Statement 3 is correct.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. English alone is the official language of the Supreme Court in India.
2. The Governor of the State, with the prior consent of the President, may authorise the use of the State’s official language in High Court proceedings.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Article 348(1) of the Constitution of India provides that all proceedings in the Supreme Court and in every High Court shall be in English language until Parliament by law otherwise provides.
• The official language of the Supreme Court is English only. Parliament is empowered to provide otherwise but it hasn't provided for any other language in the case of the Supreme Court. So, statement 1 is correct.
• The proceedings of the High Court are held in the English language. Official Languages Act. 1964 provides that the Governor of the State may, with the previous consent of the President, authorise the use of Hindi or any other official language in the proceedings of the High Court. However, judgements, decrees and orders, must continue to be in English only (until Parliament otherwise provides). So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
Relevance: The Government of Meghalaya is examining all aspects of the demand for making Khasi and Garo as official languages of Meghalaya.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. To be classified as a Classical Language, the literary tradition must be original and not borrowed from another speech community.
2. Tamil was the first language to be accorded the status of Classical Language in India.
3. All the Classical Languages of India are present in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
• In 2004, the Government of India created a new category of languages called 'classical languages. Following are the criteria set up by Ministry of Culture:
• High antiquity of its early texts/recorded history over period of 1500-2000 years;
• A body of ancient literature/texts, which is considered a valuable heritage by generations of speakers;
• The literary tradition is original and not borrowed from another speech community. So, statement 1 is correct.
• The classical language and literature being distinct from modem, there may also be a discontinuity between the classical language and its later forms or its offshoots.
• Tamil language was the first language to be given classical status in 2004. Currently, six languages enjoy the 'Classical' status: Tamil (declared in 2004). Sanskrit (2005), Kannada (2008), Telugu (2008), Malayalam (2013). and Odia (2014). So, statement 2 is correct.
• All the Classical Languages of India are present in the Eighth Schedule of the Indian Constitution along with other scheduled languages. The languages present in the Eighth Schedule are Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Hindi, Kannada, Kashmiri, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Nepali, Odia, Punjabi. Sanskrit, Sindhi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu, Bodo, Santhali, Maithili and Dogri. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: It is constituted with the previous sanction of the President.
It is chaired by the Union Home Minister and consists of members from both the Houses of Parliament.
Its objective is to review the progress made in the use of Hindi for official purposes.
It is constituted by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

q.t: With reference to the Committee of Parliament on Official Language, which one of the following statements is not correct?

so.t:
• The Committee of Parliament on Official Language was set up in 1976 under Section 4 of The Official Languages Act of 1963. Section 4 of the Act says "there shall be constituted a Committee on Official language, on a resolution to that effect being moved in either House of Parliament with the previous sanction of the President and passed by both Houses".
• The Committee is Chaired by the Union Home Minister, and has, in accordance with the provisions of the 1963 Act, 30 Members of Parliament (MPs) - 20 MPs from Lok Sabha and 10 MPs from Rajya Sabha.
• The objective of the Committee is to review the progress made in the use of Hindi for official purposes and to make recommendations to increase the use of Hindi in official communications.
• Unlike the other Parliamentary panels, the Committee of Parliament on Official Language is constituted by the Home Ministry, and it does not, like the Committees of Parliament, submit its report to Parliament. Under the provisions of the Official Languages Act, the panel submits its report to the President, who shall [then] cause the report to be laid before each House of Parliament, and sent to all the State Governments. The recommendations of the Committee are not binding in nature.
Therefore, option (d) is the answer.
Relevance: Recently, the Union Home Minister chaired the 38th meeting of the Committee of Parliament on Official Language.

q.t: In India, which of the following has the authority to establish a Joint State Public Service Commission (JSPSC) for two or more States?

so.t:
• The Constitution of India makes a provision for the establishment of a Joint State Public Service Commission (JSPSC) for two or more States. While the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) and the State Public Service Commission (SPSC) are created directly by the Constitution, a JSPSC can be created by an Act of Parliament at the request of the concerned State Legislatures.
• A JSPSC is a statutory and not a constitutional body. The Chairperson and members of the JSPSC are appointed by the President.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the NITI Aayog:
1. It was accorded Constitutional status by the 102nd Constitutional Amendment Act.
2. The Prime Minister of India is the Chairperson of NITI Aayog.
3. Its Governing Council is headed by the Union Home Minister.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
• On January 1, 2015, the NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) was established as the successor to the Planning Commission.
• It was established by an executive resolution of the Government of India (i.e., Union Cabinet). Hence, It is also neither a Constitutional body nor a Statutory body. In other words, it is a Non- Constitutional or Extra-Constitutional body (i.e. not created by the Constitution) and a non- statutory body (not created by an Act of Parliament). So, statement I is not correct.
• The Prime Minister of India is the Chairperson of NITI Aayog. The Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog is appointed by the Prime Minister. So, statement 2 is correct.
• The Governing Council comprises the Chief Ministers of all the States, Chief Ministers of Union Territories with Legislatures (i.e.. Delhi, Puducherry and Jammu and Kashmir) and Lt. Governors of other Union Territories. It is not headed by the Union Home Minister. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI), consider the following statements:
1. It functions under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
2. It was established on the recommendation of Santhanam Committee.
3. It does not require the consent of the State Government to investigate a crime in that State if it has authorisation from the Central Government.
4. It acts as the nodal agency for the extradition of fugitives from other countries to India.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
• The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) is the premier investigating police agency in India. It functions under the Department of Personnel, Ministry of Personnel, Pension & Public Grievances. So, statement 1 is not correct.
• It derives its power to investigate from the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946. However, it is not a statutory body. It was established on the recommendation of the Santhanam Committee on Prevention of Corruption (1962-1964) in 1963. So, statement 2 is correct
• The Central Government can authorise CBI to investigate a crime in a State butonly with the consent of the concerned State Government. It can suo-moto take up investigation of offences only in the Union Territories, However, the Supreme Court and High Courts can order CBI to investigate a crime anywhere in the country without the consent of the State. So, statement 3 is not correct.
• CBI plays a limited role in the extradition of fugitives. The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) is the nodal agency for the extradition of fugitives. CBI Interpol can help law enforcement agencies in tracing/locating the fugitive so that they can make a formal request for extradition of the fugitive to the MEA. So, statement 4 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t: In India, any inclusion in or exclusion from the Central List of socially and educationally backward classes can be done by:

so.t:
• The Constitution of India has not specified the classes of citizens who are to be called the socially and educationally backward classes, also known as Other Backward Classes (OBCs). The 102nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 2018 empowered the President to specify the socially and educationally backward classes in relation to a State or Union Territory.
• eIn case of a State, the President issues the notification after consultation with the Governor of the State concerned. But, any inclusion in or exclusion from the Central List of socially and educationally backward classes specified in a Presidential notification can be done only by the Parliament and not by a subsequent Presidential notification.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
Relevance: The Chairperson of the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC) informed that approximately 80 more castes are likely to be added to the Central List of Other Backward Classes (OBCs)

opt.t: Both the Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for statement I
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct but Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: The Ministers of the Union Government are not liable in any court for any act done during the tenure of their office.
Statement-II: Unlike Britain, the Ministers in India are not required to countersign the official acts of the President.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
• The Constitution does not grant any immunity to the Ministers for their official acts. But, since they are not required to countersign (as in Britain) the official acts of the President and the Governors, they are not liable in the courts for those acts. Moreover, they are not liable for the official acts done by the President and the Governors on their advice as the courts are debarred from inquiring into such advice. So, statement 2 is correct.
• However, the Ministers do not enjoy any immunity for their personal acts and can be sued for crimes as well as torts in the ordinary courts like common citizens. So, statement I is not correct.
So, Statement-l is incorrect but Statement-II is correct. Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the Bar Council of India, consider the following statements:
1. It is a statutory body established under the Advocates Act, 1961.
2. Attorney General of India acts as its ex-officio Chairperson.
3. It grants recognition to Universities whose degrees in law serve as qualifications for enrolment as an advocate.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
• Bar Council of India: It is a statutory body created by Parliament under the Advocates Act, 1961 to regulate and represent the Indian bar. So, statement I is correct.
• It consists of members elected from each State Bar Council and the Attorney General of India and the Solicitor General of India who are ex-officio members. The members from the State Bar Councils are elected for a period of five years. The Council elects its own Chairperson and Vice- Chairperson for a period of two years from among its members. So, statement 2 is not correct.
• It performs the regulatory function by prescribing standards of professional conduct and etiquette and by exercising disciplinary jurisdiction over the bar. It also sets standards for legal education and grants recognition to Universities whose degrees in law will serve as qualifications for enrolment as an advocate. So, statement 3 is correct.
• So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
Relevance: Recently, a lawyer has approached the Kerala High Court challenging the Order issued by the Bar Council of India (BCI) interdicting State Bar Councils from initiating the process of election for constituting a new Bar council.

q.t:
With reference to Law Commission of India, consider the following statements:
1. It is established by an order of Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice.
2. It submits its final report to the President.
3. Four Law Commissions were established in the 19th century during the British regime.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
• Statement I is correct: Law Commission of India is a non-statutory advisory body. It is established by the order of the Central Government from time to time for a fixed tenure.
• The order is issued by the Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice
• Statement 2 is incorrect: Once the data and views/suggestions are assimilated, the Commission evaluates them and the information is utilized for appropriate incorporation in the report which is written under the guidance of the Hon'ble Chairman, Members and Member-Secretary of the Commission. It is then subjected to close scrutiny by the full Commission in the meeting. Once the Report and summary are finalized, The law Commission of India submits its report to the Union Law and Justice Ministry.
• Statement 3 is correct: During the British regime, four Law Commissions were established in the 19th century. They made a significant contribution towards the enrichment of the Indian Statute Book. They recommended a variety of legislations on the pattern of the then prevailing English laws adopted to Indian conditions. The Indian Penal Code, the Criminal Procedure Code, the Civil Procedure Code, the Indian Contract Act, the Indian Evidence Act, the Transfer of Property Act and some other laws are the products of these four Commissions.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the National Commission for Women, consider the following statements:
1. The chairperson and members of the commission are appointed by the president.
2. It investigates and examines all matters relating to the safeguards provided for women under the Constitution.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 is incorrect The National Commission for Women (NCW) is a statutory body formed by the Central government through the National Commission for Women Act of 1990. The Central government appoints the chairperson and five members, and a member-secretary is also nominated by the Central Government.
Statement 2 is correct: The NCW is tasked with investigating and scrutinizing all aspects pertaining to the safeguards prescribed for women in the Constitution and other legislations. Additionally, it addresses complaints and initiates action on its own accord concerning issues related to the violation of women's rights.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following events
1. Establishment of Planning commission of India
2. Appointment of first official language commission.
3. Creation of National Investigation agency.
4. Establishment of CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation)
Arrange the above events in chronological order:

so.t:
• Planning Commission of India was established in the year 15th March 1950.
• The first official language commission was appointed by President under the chairmanship of BG Kher in 1955.
• The National Investigation agency was constituted in 2009 under the provisions of the National Investigation agency act, 2008 (NIA Act).
• The CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) was set up in 1963 by a resolution of Ministry of Home affairs.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. In specific situations, the Election Commission of India holds the authority to conduct elections for panchayats and municipalities.
2. The State Election Commissions work under the overall supervision of the Election Commission of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (STs) is a constitutional body. Some of its features are:
• Statement I is correct. The Chairperson, The Vice Chairperson and the Members shall be appointed from amongst persons of ability, integrity and standing who have had a record of selfless service to the cause of justice for the Scheduled Tribes. The Chairperson shall be appointed from amongst eminent socio-political workers belonging to the Scheduled Tribe. At least two members out of Vice-Chairperson and all other Members shall be belonging to the Scheduled Tribes. At least one other Member shall be appointed from amongst women.
• Statement 2 is incorect. The Commission has the powers of a civil court. It does not have power of civil court in the matter of punishing. It is vested power in the matters of investigation and inquiry.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t: The term “Flying Bees” got associated with which of the following associations?

so.t: The term "Flying Bees" is associated with ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations). This metaphor symbolizes the interconnectedness, collective efforts, and industrious nature of ASEAN member nations working together toward economic and social progress, much like bees in a hive.

q.t: Which of the following freedoms was not mentioned by Roosevelt?

so.t:
Franklin D. Roosevelt outlined the Four Freedoms in his famous speech on January 6, 1941. These freedoms are as follows:
Freedom of Speech
The right to express opinions freely without fear of censorship or restraint.
Freedom of Worship
The right to practice any religion or no religion at all.
Freedom from Want
The right to an adequate standard of living, including access to food, shelter, and employment.
Freedom from Fear
The right to live in a world free from violence, war, and oppression.

q.t: Who is the author of the book “Escape from Freedom”?

so.t: Erich FrommErich Fromm authored "Escape from Freedom" in 1941, a seminal work exploring the psychological impact of freedom and how individuals often find it overwhelming, leading them to escape through submission to authoritarianism, conformity, or destructiveness. The book examines the tension between individual liberty and societal structures, particularly in the context of the rise of totalitarian regimes like Nazism, making Fromm a key figure in understanding the psychological roots of freedom and its challenges.

opt.t: Globalization gives emphasis on market economy
Globalization gives rise to international institutions to supervise economy
Globalization tends to perpetuate parochial culture

q.t: Which one of the following is incorrect statement?

so.t: Globalization is a process of interaction and integration among the people, companies, and governments of different nations, a process driven by international trade and investment and aided by information technology. It did not perpetuate parochial culture.

q.t: Democratic peace theory has been largely associated with the writings of:

so.t: The Democratic Peace Theory states that countries with liberal democratic forms of government are less likely to go to war with one another than those with other forms of government. Democratic peace theory has been largely associated with the writings of Michael Doyle and Bruce Russett.

q.t: 'Open Door Policy' was associated with ?

so.t: Open Door policy, statement of principles initiated by the United States in 1899 and 1900 for the protection of equal privileges among countries trading with China and in support of Chinese territorial and administrative integrity.

q.t: Para Diplomacy is/was part of India's Foreign Policy under ?

so.t: Para Diplomacy as it is conducted by sub-state governments introduces the idea of decentralization of political power to make regional governments prominent actors in the international sphere.

q.t: Peaceful Nuclear Explosion (PNE) was a policy of the Government under?

so.t: Peaceful Nuclear Explosion: On the morning of May 18, 1974, a nuclear device was detonated in the Rajasthan desert near Pokhran, India. This event came as a shock to the entire world. Work on a nuclear fission device had been authorized by Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi on September 7, 1972

q.t:
With reference to the Polygraph Test, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is a lie detector test based on the physiological responses of a person.
2. The results of the tests cannot be considered to be confessions.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Both the statements are correct.
What is the Polygraph Test?
● The Polygraph Test is commonly known as a lie detector test.
● It is based on the assumption that physiological responses (heartbeat, changes in breathing, sweating, etc.) triggered when a person is lying are different from what they would be otherwise.
Are the results of the tests admissible as evidence?
● In ‘Selvi & Ors vs State of Karnataka & Anr’ (2010) the Supreme Court said,
that the results of the tests cannot be considered to be “confessions”.

q.t:
With reference to the Square Kilometre Array Observatory (SKAO),consider the following statements:
1. It is a Joint project between ISRO and NASA.
2. It consists of the two telescopes at radio-quiet sites in South Africa and Australia.
3. India will contribute in the development and operation of the Telescope Manager element, the “neural network”.
How many statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 not correct and Statement 2 and 3 are correct.
Square Kilometre Array Observatory
● The SKA Observatory is an intergovernmental organization bringing together 14 countries;
Australia, Canada, China, France, Germany, India, Italy, New Zealand, Spain, South Africa,
Sweden, Switzerland, The Netherlands and the United Kingdom.
● Headquarters: United Kingdom
● Mandate: Its aim is to build and operate cutting-edge radio telescopes to transform the
understanding of the Universe.
o It will explore the formation and evolution of galaxies, fundamental physics in extreme
environments and the origins of life.
● The observatory consists of the two telescopes at radio-quiet sites in South Africa and Australia, and associated facilities to support the operations of the telescopes.
Indian Engagement in SKAO
● India, through the Pune-based National Centre for Radio Astrophysics (NCRA) has been involved in the development of SKA since its inception in the 1990s.
● India’s main contribution is in the development, and operation, of the Telescope Manager
element, the “neural network” or the software that will make the telescope work.

q.t:
With reference to the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT), which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It operates within the 110-1,460 MegaHertz frequency range.
2. It was among the top radio telescopes that detected the nano-hertz gravitational waves for the first time.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Both the statements are correct.
Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT)
● It is operated by the National Centre for Radio Astrophysics (NCRA), a part of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research.
● GMRT is the world’s largest and most sensitive radio telescope operating within the 110-1,460 MegaHertz frequency range.
● Significance: GMRT was among the six top radio telescopes used to enable the detection of the nano-hertz gravitational waves for the first time.
o Such waves are expected to originate from a large number of dancing monster black hole
pairs, several million times heavier than the Sun.

q.t:
With reference to the Agnipath scheme, consider the following statements:
1. It is a short-term recruitment scheme for the Indian Army also called Tour of Duty.
2. All the recruits from a batch are retained for regular service.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● Statement 1 is correct: Introduced in 2022, the Agnipath scheme — also called the Tour of Duty
scheme is a short-term recruitment scheme for the Indian Army.
● Statement 2 is not correct: Under the policy, soldiers — called ‘Agniveers’ — are recruited for four
years, at the end of which only 25 percent of recruits from a batch are retained for regular service.

opt.t: Improve water quality of Ganga river for irrigation purposes.
Develop hydroelectric power projects along the Ganges River.
Rejuvenate and conserve the entire Ganga river basin.
Attract tourism and promote riverfront development.

q.t: The primary objective of the National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) is to:

so.t:
National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG):
● NMCG was registered as a society on 12th August 2011 under the Societies Registration Act 1860.
● It acted as the implementation arm of National Ganga River Basin Authority(NGRBA) which was constituted under the provisions of the Environment (Protection) Act (EPA),1986.
o NGRBA has since been dissolved with effect from the 7th October 2016, consequent to the constitution of National Ganga Council (National Council for Rejuvenation, Protection and Management of River Ganga).
● Parent body: Ministry of Jal Shakti
● Mandate:
o Rejuvenate and conserve the entire Ganga river basin.
o To ensure effective abatement of pollution and rejuvenation of the river Ganga by adopting a river basin approach.
o To maintain minimum ecological flows in the river Ganga with the aim of ensuring water quality and environmentally sustainable development.

q.t: SVAMITVA (Survey of villages and mapping with improvised technology in village areas) scheme, is an initiative of which of the following ministries?

so.t:
Ministry of Panchayati Raj
About
● SVAMITVA Scheme of the Ministry of Panchayati Raj is a Central Sector Scheme (fully funded by the central government) that has significantly contributed to the empowerment of rural communities in India.
● The scheme ensures accurate demarcation of land parcels, thereby mitigating disputes and providing individuals with property records of rights i.e. SVAMITVA Property Cards
● It facilitates monetization of properties, enabling bank loans and comprehensive village-level planning.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the PRERANA program.
1. It is an experiential learning for developing leadership qualities in students of class VI to XII.
2. It is a week-long residential program that has been launched by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Statements 1 and 2 are not correct : PRERANA program is a week-long residential program for selected students of class IX to XII. The Ministry of Education has launched the ‘Prerana ‘program.
● It is an experiential and inspirational learning program for students with the best-in-class technology where heritage meets innovation.
● A batch of 20 selected students (10 boys and 10 girls) will attend the program every week from various parts of the country.
o It will run from a Vernacular School, established in 1888, in one of the oldest living cities of India, Vadnagar, district Mehsana, Gujarat.
It will feature yoga, mindfulness, and meditation sessions, followed by experiential learning, thematic sessions, and hands-on interesting learning activities.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. India tested its first nuclear weapon in 1974, becoming the sixth country to detonate a nuclear weapon.
2. India is a member of Missile Technology Control Regime (MTCR) and Nuclear Suppliers Group (NSG).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 is correct:
● Nuclear Power of India: India tested its first nuclear weapon in 1974, becoming the sixth country to detonate a nuclear weapon, and it has approximately 164 nuclear warheads, and has land-based, sea-based and air-launch nuclear capabilities.
● India had declared a No First Use policy, which means they have vowed to never use nuclear weapons first in a conflict.
o However, as of August 2019, India said they are reconsidering this policy.
Statement 2 is not correct:
● India is pursuing membership to the Nuclear Suppliers Group (NSG) and became a member of the Missile Technology Control Regime (MTCR)  in 2016.
○ India has not signed the  Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty nor The Comprehensive Test Ban Treaty.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Name of Military Exercises : Related Countries
1. Desert : Iran
2. Sampriti : Bangladesh
3. Khanjar : Egypt
4. Garuda Shakti : Thailand
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?

so.t:
In News: The Joint Military Exercise 'Desert Cyclone 2024' will be held from January 2 to January 15 in Rajasthan.
Pairs 1,3 and 4 are incorrectly matched : Exercise 'Desert Cyclone 2024 is the Joint Military Exercise between India and UAE
Kyrgyzstan: Ex KHANJAR
● Indonesia: Ex GARUDA SHAKTI
Only Pair 2 is correctly matched : Bangladesh: SAMPRITI.

q.t:
With reference to the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (1966), consider the following statements:
1. India is a signatory.
2. It follows one of the provisions made in the Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP) of the Indian Constitution.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 is correct: The International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (1966): Article 17 says that ‘no one shall be subjected to arbitrary or unlawful interference with his privacy, family, home, and correspondence nor unlawful attacks on his honour and reputation’.
● India is a signatory and party to the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.
Statement 2 is correct: According to Article 51(c) of the Constitution (DPSP), international conventions must be respected unless they conflict with domestic laws.

q.t:
With reference to the Chabahar port, consider the following statements:
1. It is located on the Gulf of Oman and is the only oceanic port of Iran.
2. It provides India with direct sea access to Afghanistan and Central Asia.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Both the statements are correct: Iran's Chabahar port is located on the Gulf of Oman and is the only oceanic port of the country.
● Geopolitical Significance: Chabahar Port is strategically located at the crossroads of South Asia, Central Asia, and the Middle East. It provides India with direct sea access to Afghanistan and Central Asia, bypassing Pakistan.
● India can bypass Pakistan in transporting goods to Afghanistan.

opt.t: It is a political system characterised by the division of power between the two Houses of Union Legislature.
It is a political system that involves giving dual citizenship to citizens compulsorily.
It is the system of having an elected and non-hereditary Head of the State.
It is a system of government in which power is divided between a central authority and various constituent units of the country.

q.t: Which of the following statements rightly explains the term ‘federalism’?

so.t:
Option a is incorrect: The presence of two Houses in a Legislature is termed as Bicameralism.
However, both Unitary (like the United Kingdom) and Federal (like United States of America) polities may have a Bicameral Legislature. Hence this is not a correct definition of Federalism. Generally, these two Houses have different powers and functions, with one being more powerful than the other. These two Houses are present at the same level of polity (such as both at the Centre like Rajya Sabha & Lok Sabha, or both at state level like Vidhan Sabha and Vidhan Parishad).
Option b is incorrect: Dual citizenship means that a same individual is recognized as a citizen, with some intrinsic rights, by different sets of polities. It may mean that a person can hold citizenship of two different countries (like Canada allows), or it may also mean that a person is recognized as a citizen of both the nation and the state in which he is born/ resides (such as in the USA). However, grant of Dual Citizenship to citizens does not necessarily mean that a polity is Federal (as Canada is a Unitary state, while USA is a federal state, likewise India is a federal state, but it only has Single citizenship). Hence this statement is incorrect.
Option c is incorrect: A polity wherein the Head of the State is an elected representative (not a hereditary head such as a monarch), is known as a Republic, not a Federal Polity. Hence this statement is incorrect.
Option d is correct: A feature that can be said to be constant throughout all polities characterised as Federal, is that there is a division of power across various tiers (levels of government) - such as Centre, Regional/ State, Local, etc. Federalism is a system of government in which power is divided between a central authority and various constituent units of the country. A federation has minimum two levels of government. All these levels of governments enjoy their power somewhat independent of the other.

q.t:
With reference to the differences between Federal features of USA and India, consider the following statements:
1. The allocation of seats in upper houses of legislatures of both countries is on then basis of the population of each State.
2. While the states in America have been guaranteed territorial integrity, the states in India have not.
3. While there is a single constitution in India, in America each state has its own constitution.
Which of the statements given above is/ are correct?

so.t:
Both India and the United States of America are federal polity in structure. This means that there is a division of power between a central and a regional government. However, there are significant differences in many features and structures relating to Federalism in both countries.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Both US and India have a bicameral legislature. The upper and the lower houses of US legislature are called as the House of Senate and the House of Representatives respectively, and the Indian Parliament has Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha as its Lower and Upper house respectively. The allocation of seats in Rajya Sabha is made on the basis of the population of each State. However, in USA, all states are given equal representation in the Senate irrespective of their population. USA has 50 states and the Senate has 100 members–2 from each state. Statement 2 is correct: The United States of America was created as a result of an agreement among recently liberated states under British colonisation in North America. So, their constitution was designed such that the autonomy and powers of the constituent states were preserved to a great extent. So according to the American constitution, the Central government cannot create or merge states or alter their boundaries with a simple legislation, if at all, thus almost guaranteeing their existence in perpetuity. Hence the states in America have absolute territorial integrity. On the other hand, in India, the constitution created a federal polity with a bias for a strong centre. So as per Article 2, 3 and 4, the Central Parliament can easily create or destroy or merge a state, or alter its boundaries or name, by passing a simple legislation with ordinary procedure (simple majority), without the consent of the states involved. Thus, states in India, do not have territorial integrity. Rather, the Indian nation is the one whose integrity is paramount, as no state can declare that it is seceding. Hence this statement is correct. Statement 3 is correct: Despite being a federal polity, India has a single constitution, that governs governance at both the centre and in the states. This was done by the makers of the constitution to foster feelings of fraternity, brotherhood and unity among Indians, who are very diverse and were very loosely united at the time of independence. On the other hand, since America was formed as a result of agreement between states coming together, they retained their power by opting to run the government according to their own constitution. Hence each state in America, has its own constitution in addition to a common constitution which is applicable to the entire USA. Hence this statement is correct.

q.t:
With reference to the changes in the territory of states/Union Territories in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Indian Constitution requires Parliament to get the consent of all state(s) involved in creation/ merger of states.
2. A Parliamentary bill providing for alteration in the boundaries of a Union Territory must be referred to the legislature of the concerned Union Territory.
Which of the statements given above is/ are correct?

so.t: The constitution of India describes India as a Union in Article 1. Despite having a federal structure, where the state governments have reasonable autonomy within the spheres allocated to them by the constitution, the Indian constitution has a bias towards a strong Centre. So as is often used to describe Indian polity, the constitution is federal in structure but Unitary in spirit. This is visible in various provisions as well. Statement 1 is incorrect: As per Articles 2 and 3, the Parliament at the Centre can create new states, or make adjustments in existing states (size, boundaries, name, etc). They mention that the Parliament does not need to gain the consent of the state in question, for any of the activities (not just name or boundary change) mentioned before. Hence this statement is incorrect. The Parliament can unilaterally create or merge or change any territorial feature of any state. To do so, only a simple legislation requiring ordinary/ simple majority will be sufficient, and it would not be considered to be an amendment under Article 368 (as per Article 4). Statement 2 is incorrect: A bill contemplating the changes in the area/boundaries/name of a state can be introduced in the Parliament only with the prior recommendation of the President. Also, before recommending the bill, the President has to refer the same to the state legislature concerned for expressing its views within a specified period. But, in case of a union territory, no reference need be made to the concerned legislature to ascertain its views and the Parliament can itself take any action as it deems fit.

q.t:
Which of the following are provisions/ features of the Indian constitution that creates a federation with strong Unitary bias?
1. President's rule under Article 356.
2. Language to be used in the Legislature under Article 210.
3. Governor’s power to reserve any Bill for consideration of the President.
4. Jurisdiction of the Union in relation to territories outside India.
5. Power of Parliament to legislate with respect to a matter in the State List. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t: The constitution of India describes India as a Union in Article 1. Despite having a federal structure, where the state governments have reasonable autonomy within the spheres allocated to them by the constitution, the Indian constitution has a bias towards a strong Centre. So as is often used to describe Indian polity, the constitution is federal in structure but Unitary in spirit. This is visible in various provisions as well. Option 1 is correct: Article 356 of Part XVIII of the Constitution relates to the provisions governing declaration of Emergency, specifically declaration of President’s Rule in a state (State/ Constitutional emergency). This is a type of emergency that is declared when the Governor of a state feels that the government of the state cannot be run in accordance with constitutional provisions. It results in the control of that particular state going directly into the hands of the Centre. This means that the executive decisions relating to that state are taken by the President on advice of the central Council of Ministers, and the legislation for that state is done by the Parliament, not the state legislature. Thus, this provision gives extraordinary power to the Union, while diminishing the autonomy of the state considerably. Hence this option is correct. Option 2 is incorrect: Article 210 of Part VI of the Indian Constitution is a provision relating to the procedures and working of the State Legislatures. It details out which all languages may be used to conduct the proceedings in the State Legislature. It has no bearing on Centre-State relations. Hence it is an incorrect option. Option 3 is correct: Article 201 of Part VI of the Indian constitution relates to the proceedings in the State Legislature, specifically with respect to the passage of Bills and other legislations. This provision gives a special power to the Governor, to reserve any Bill submitted for approval by the State Legislature, for consideration of the President. Doing so, shifts the ultimate destiny of such a Bill to the Central Executive (President advised by the Central Council of Ministers). After this step, the passage of the Bill is dependent upon the wishes of the Central Executive, giving it greater power than State Legislative. Hence this option is correct. Option 4 is incorrect: Article 260 of Part VI pertains to the jurisdiction of the Centre to administer and matters related to territories outside India. This is not related to giving the Union overwhelming powers with respect to states. Hence this option is incorrect. Option 5 is correct: Article 249 of Part VI pertains to the special provision that allows the Parliament to legislate on any matter in the State List (in Schedule VII) (which is normally considered in domain of state legislatures, and hence out of bound for the Union, as it should be under Federal principle of Distribution of Power). Although a Special Majority (2/3 members present & voting) in the Rajya Sabha is required to authorise this, still having such a provision provides a way for the Union to exert power over the states. Hence this option is incorrect.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Lokpal and Lokayukta:
1. There is no uniformity of powers of Lokayukta of different states.
2. Lokpal cannot Suo motu proceed against any public servant.
3. The Prime Minister has been kept completely outside the purview of the Lokpal.
Which of the statements given above is/ are correct?

so.t: The Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013 provided for the establishment of Lokpal for the Union and Lokayukta for States. The Act allows setting up of anti-corruption ombudsman called Lokpal at the Centre and Lokayukta at the State-level. They perform the function of an "ombudsman” and inquire into allegations of corruption against certain public functionaries and for related matters. Statement 1 is correct: As per the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013, every state has the autonomy to frame their own laws, thus the Lokayukta’s powers vary from state to state on various aspects, such as tenure, and the need for sanction to prosecute officials. Some states such as Rajasthan, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Maharashtra have created the Lokayukta as well as Up-Lokayukta, while some other states have created only the Lokayukta. Statement 2 is correct: Unless a complaint has been made, Lokpal cannot Suo motu proceed against any public servant. As per the Lokpal Act, the "complaint" means a complaint, made in such form as may be prescribed, alleging that a public servant has committed an offence punishable under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988. Jurisdiction of Lokpal includes Group A, B, C and D officers and officials of Central Government. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Prime Minister has been brought under the purview of the Lokpal with subject matter exclusions and specific process for handling complaints against the Prime Minister. The Lokpal has jurisdiction to inquire into allegations of corruption against anyone who is or has been Prime Minister, or a Minister in the Union government, or a Member of Parliament.

q.t:
Which of the properties/ income of the states among the following are exempted from Central taxation?
1. Buildings and the offices of state government departments.
2. Equity & Debt Investments made by the state government.
3. Premises of state Public Sector Enterprises.
4. Machines imported for road construction, or tunnel digging by the state government.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t: Statement 1 is correct: Any property/ income/ asset which is used by the state governments to carry out their sovereign functions (carry out the roles and duties of governance entrusted to them by the constitution) are exempt from taxation by the Central government. Since the buildings, housing and the offices of various government departments are used to provide governance services to the people in a state, such properties owned by the state are exempt from any taxation. Hence this Statement is correct. Statement 2 is correct: The government, both at Centre an in the states, makes investments in various assets like equities and debt funds, through proper channels in order to be able to raise resources for their budgets, so that they may meet revenue expenses (welfare expenses like subsidies, pensions, salaries, etc) as well as capital expenditure (creating new infrastructure, etc). So, such assets, although normally taxed, are exempt from central taxation (such as securities transaction tax, dividend distribution tax, capital gains tax, etc), as it is in the service of providing public services (a sovereign function) by the state governments. Hence this Statement is correct. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Constitution mentions that property or income of corporations or companies owned by the state government (such as state PSEs like UP Power Corporation Ltd, etc) are not exempt from Central taxation, even if they are in the service of the public. Hence this Statement is incorrect. Statement 4 is incorrect: The Supreme Court in an advisory opinion in 1963, held that the immunity granted to a state in respect of Central taxation does not extend to Excise or Custom duties. This means that if a state imports anything, even if it is for a public purpose, like machines for digging tunnels or laying roads, from abroad, it will have to pay custom duty (which is a central tax). Hence this Statement is incorrect

q.t:
With reference to Centre-state relations during emergency, consider the following statements:
1. During National Emergency, President of India can cancel the transfer of finances from the Centre to the states.
2. During State Emergency, all the Money bills passed by the state legislature automatically gets reserved for the consideration of the President.
3. During Financial Emergency, Centre can give directions to the states to reduce the salaries of any class of persons serving in the state.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t: The Centre-state financial relations (under Articles 268 to 293 in Part XII of the Constitution) in normal times undergo changes during emergencies. Statement 1 is correct: While the proclamation of national emergency (under Article 352) is in operation, the president can modify the constitutional distribution of revenues between the Centre and the states. This means that the President can either reduce or cancel the transfer of finances (both tax sharing and grants-in-aid) from the Centre to the states. Such modification continues till the end of the financial year in which the emergency ceases to operate. Statement 2 is incorrect: Article 365 says that whenever a state fails to comply with or to give effect to any direction from the Centre, it will be lawful for the president to hold that a situation has arisen in which the government of the state cannot be carried on in accordance with the provisions of the Constitution. However, there is no provision of automatic reservation of Money Bill passed by state government to President of India. Under Financial Emergency, Centre can give directions to the states to reserve all money bills and other financial bills for the consideration of the President. Statement 3 is correct: While the proclamation of financial emergency (under Article 360) is in operation, the Centre can give directions to the states: (i) to observe the specified canons of financial propriety; (ii) to reduce the salaries and allowances of all class of persons serving in the state; and (iii) to reserve all money bills and other financial bills for the consideration of the President.

q.t:
With reference to law making power in India, consider the following statements:
1. In India, only Parliament is authorized to make extraterritorial legislation.
2. The power to make laws with respect to levy of residuary taxes is vested in respective state legislature.
3. Both the Parliament and State legislatures has the power to make laws regarding Goods and Services Tax.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t: Statement 1 is correct: The Parliament alone can make ‘extraterritorial legislation’. The parliament may enact legislation that has an impact beyond the territories in the interest of the people who are native of India. Thus, the laws of the Parliament are also applicable to the Indian citizens and their property in any part of the world. Statement 2 is incorrect: The power to make laws with respect to residuary subjects (i.e., the matters which are not enumerated in any of the three lists) is vested in the Parliament.
